headline: Anthropic valuation discrepancies raise questions about AI vendor reliability
body: Anthropic has disclosed conflicting valuations in public statements versus court filings, with the company citing $5B in legal proceedings while promoting $19B-$30B figures publicly. For healthcare AI implementations, vendor financial transparency and stability directly impact your ability to rely on critical LLM infrastructure for clinical decision support and EHR integration. These valuation inconsistencies, combined with reported API service disruptions and support escalation failures, suggest governance gaps that should concern health systems evaluating long-term partnerships with frontier AI vendors. When deploying AI into value-based care workflows or sensitive patient data environments, vendor accountability and operational reliability are non-negotiable prerequisites. Healthcare organizations should conduct deeper due diligence on vendor financial disclosures and support infrastructure before committing to production deployments.
